title: lewisia rediviva var. rediviva
intro: Lewisia rediviva var. rediviva, also known as the bitterroot, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of western North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bitterroot has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of the plant are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal benefits.
